commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a WSL2-only clipboard attempt that pipes stdin through `bash -lc 'cat | /mnt/c/Windows/System32/clip.exe'` and extends clipboard tests for token argv handling and non-WSL fallback order.

PR surface: Source +4, Tests +40. Total +44 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence live reproduction was established in this review. The current source and linked report make the WSL2 exec path plausible, but the successful before/after WSL2 clipboard round trip is still missing.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] This draft maintainer PR needs author or maintainer follow-up for the shell-startup security finding and the unresolved live WSL2 proof gap; it is not a close or replacement-branch candidate.

target.title: fix(infra): bridge WSL clipboard through shell
target.body:
## Summary

- route WSL2 clipboard copies through a static shell bridge to `/mnt/c/Windows/System32/clip.exe`
- keep dashboard/token URLs on stdin instead of shell argv
- preserve the existing macOS, Linux, native Windows, PowerShell, and non-WSL2 fallback order

Fixes #88080.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/clipboard.test.ts src/infra/wsl.test.ts src/commands/dashboard.links.test.ts` - passed, 30 focused tests
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/infra/clipboard.ts src/infra/clipboard.test.ts` - passed
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/infra/clipboard.ts src/infra/clipboard.test.ts` - passed
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` - passed
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` - clean, no accepted/actionable findings, overall patch correct 0.86
- WSL2 Crabbox proof attempt: `run_3f2c9daafc72`, Azure `windows/wsl2`, class `standard`, market `on-demand`, slug `wsl-clipboard-88365`; coordinator lease allocation did not return a lease after about 8 minutes and the current-task local process was stopped. `crabbox list --provider azure --json` did not show a `wsl-clipboard-88365` lease afterward.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: WSL2 dashboard clipboard copies no longer rely on direct non-shell `clip.exe` argv execution. The WSL2 path uses `bash -lc 'cat | /mnt/c/Windows/System32/clip.exe'` and passes the copied dashboard URL through stdin.

Real environment tested: local focused unit/link tests in a Codex worktree; autoreview of the committed branch. A live Azure Windows WSL2 Crabbox run was attempted but never received a lease from the coordinator.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/clipboard.test.ts src/infra/wsl.test.ts src/commands/dashboard.links.test.ts`; formatter, lint, diff-check, and autoreview commands listed above. The WSL2 Crabbox attempt requested an Azure `windows/wsl2` lease and would have piped a generated probe string through `/mnt/c/Windows/System32/clip.exe`, then read it back with `powershell.exe Get-Clipboard`.

Evidence after fix: `copyToClipboard` now prepends the shell bridge only when `isWSL2Sync()` is true. The WSL2 regression test asserts the token URL is passed via stdin, not argv. A separate fallback-order test asserts non-WSL2 environments still try `pbcopy`, `xclip`, `wl-copy`, then `clip.exe` without the WSL2 bridge.

Observed result after fix: focused tests, formatting, lint, diff-check, and autoreview all passed on commit `4dd1040134d39c4c2bec7fa44b888dbaa2379596`. Live WSL2 readback is still blocked on runner provisioning, not on a failing clipboard readback.

What was not tested: successful live WSL2 clipboard round-trip on a real Windows host; Azure Crabbox WSL2 lease allocation did not produce a VM/lease for this run.
